content: Keep receiving the above error when trying to send a response to the mailing list via git. Anyone here hit this error before? Sending emails in general works (also via git), so I suspect it has something to do with one of the addresses in the rather large list of recipients.
The git send-email returns it as an error, or do you get an email sent to you containing that?
It's probably specific to your email server...
Could also be the contents that are invalid in some way. For example, there could be non-ascii characters, or the subject could be really long.

I've had long subjects get rejected when trying to git-send-mail the .eml file I got with b4 send -o, but have the same email be accepted when I git-send-mail the same patch with the same subject when the file came from git-format-patch.

Alice Ryhl said:

Could also be the contents that are invalid in some way. For example, there could be non-ascii characters, or the subject could be really long.

I was able to send the same text to another address of mine. So this can't be the culprit.

Alice Ryhl said:

It's probably specific to your email server...

Yea, it's probably the way of my email provider to tell me that it is not suited for participation in upstream development.

Have you got recommendations for a provider that supports bring-your-own-domain and works well with the mailing lists?

Well, I send all my patchsets via @google.com with an internal tool, so... As for my personal email, I've had no problems making replies via my FastMail account. I have yet to try sending a patchset via fastmail, but I would expect it to work.

Alice Ryhl said:

Well, I send all my patchsets via @google.com with an internal tool, so... As for my personal email, I've had no problems making replies via my FastMail account. I have yet to try sending a patchset via fastmail, but I would expect it to work.

I can confirm that Fastmail works fine.
